query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Usurp', 'To seize power from another, usually by illegitimate means', 'The author hoped the new policy would usurp tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Usury', 'Unethical money lending', 'Her usury became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Utilitarian', 'Having regard to usefulness rather than beauty', 'Critics argued that the plan might utilitarian the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Utility', 'The state or condition of being useful, usefulness', 'Scholars noted a striking utility between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Utmost', 'Of the greatest or highest degree, quantity or the like', 'The essay highlights the utmost of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Utopia', 'An ideal place or state', 'The essay highlights the utopia of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Uxorious', 'Overly devoted or submissive to one''s wife', 'The author hoped the new policy would uxorious tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vacate', 'To move out of a dwelling, either by choice or by eviction', 'Critics argued that the plan might vacate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vaccinate', 'Treat with a vaccine to produce immunity against a disease', 'Over time, the community learned to vaccinate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vacillate', 'To waver between choices', '“He vacillated between two career paths.”', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vacuity', 'Absent of thought or intelligence', 'Scholars noted a striking vacuity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vacuous', 'Having or showing a lack of thought or intelligence', 'The passage presents a vacuous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vacuum', 'A space entirely devoid of matter', 'Scholars noted a striking vacuum between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vagabond', 'Wandering from place to place without any settled home', 'Critics argued that the plan might vagabond the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vagary', 'An erratic notion or action', 'Scholars noted a striking vagary between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vagrant', 'A person who wanders and has no permanent home or employment', 'The essay highlights the vagrant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vague', 'Of uncertain or ill-defined character or meaning', 'The reviewer described the tone as vague, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vainglorious', 'Filled with excessive pride over one''s own achievements', 'What seemed vainglorious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vainglory', 'Excessive vanity', 'The essay highlights the vainglory of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vale', 'Valley', 'Scholars noted a striking vale between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Valediction', 'A speech made when leaving or parting company', 'The essay highlights the valediction of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Valedictorian', 'The student who gives the farewell address at graduation', 'The essay highlights the valedictorian of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Valedictory', 'Bidding good-bye', 'Scholars noted a striking valedictory between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Valiant', 'Boldly courageous', 'The essay highlights the valiant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Valid', 'Well grounded or justifiable, pertinent', 'Scholars noted a striking valid between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Validate', 'To confirm or endorse something as accurate or legitimate', 'Over time, the community learned to validate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Valorous', 'Courageous', 'The reviewer described the tone as valorous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vanguard', 'The foremost division or the front part of an army', 'The essay highlights the vanguard of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vantage', 'A position affording some advantage or commanding view', 'The essay highlights the vantage of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vapid', 'Lacking or having lost life, sharpness or flavour', 'Scholars noted a striking vapid between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vaporizer', 'A device with a heating element, used to vaporize a liquid', 'Over time, the community learned to vaporizer its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Variable', 'Likely to change', '“The weather here is highly variable.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Variant', 'Tending to change or alter', 'The author hoped the new policy would variant tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Variegat', 'Varied in appearance or color', 'The essay highlights the variegat of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Variegate', 'To make varied in appearance', 'The author hoped the new policy would variegate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vassal', 'A person granted land in exchange for loyalty to a superior', 'Critics argued that the plan might vassal the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vaudeville', 'A theatrical piece of light or amusing character', 'The essay highlights the vaudeville of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Veer', 'To change from one course or direction to another', 'Critics argued that the plan might veer the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vegetal', 'Pertaining to vegetables or other plants', 'Critics argued that the plan might vegetal the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vegetate', 'To grow or sprout', 'Critics argued that the plan might vegetate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vegetative', 'Growing or developing as or like plants', 'The reviewer described the tone as vegetative, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vehemence', 'An intense concentration, force or power', 'Her vehemence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vehement', 'Showing strong feeling; forceful and intense', 'The passage presents a vehement portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Veil', 'Covered or concealed by a veil', 'Scholars noted a striking veil between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Velocity', 'Rapidity of motion or operation', 'The essay highlights the velocity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Velvety', 'Like velvet (but not velvet), soft, smooth', 'Her velvety became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Venal', 'Willing to sell one''s influence,  in return for a bribe', 'Over time, the community learned to venal its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vendetta', 'Any prolonged and bitter feud, rivalry or contention', 'Her vendetta became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vendible', 'Able to be bought, sold, or traded', 'Over time, the community learned to vendible its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vendition', 'The act of vending or selling, sale', 'The essay highlights the vendition of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
